libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: Configure generated tests that exercise OGNL reflective/member-access paths to run with JVM args `--add-opens=java.base/java.lang=ALL-UNNAMED` and `--add-opens=java.base/java.util=ALL-UNNAMED`. Do not enable `ognl.security.manager` globally; it is an optional OGNL sandbox feature and may be fragile on newer JDKs where `SecurityManager` is disabled or removed.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #7430 [Automation] java run fails for ognl:ognl:3.3.5; label=fails-java-run; body_chars=8015

risks: The existing 3.2.21 scaffold only opens `java.lang`; upstream OGNL 3.3.5 opens both `java.lang` and `java.util` on JDK 17+.
SecurityManager-oriented tests may fail on JDK 25 for platform reasons rather than missing reachability metadata.

If a generated scaffold somehow disables transitive dependencies, OGNL will fail because `org.javassist:javassist` is required, but the normal Gradle dependency on `ognl:ognl:3.3.5` should resolve it automatically.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: OGNL 3.3.5 has no external service requirement and its only runtime dependency, Javassist, is declared transitively, but its own JDK 17+ test profile opens JDK modules for reflective access.
